---
title: "Week 9 — Multiple / logistic regression interpretation"
toc: true
---

> **Status:** placeholder. Real content will be developed during the
> course build.

## Big question

What changes when we account for other variables, and how do we
interpret a model that predicts a yes/no outcome?

## Day-by-day

### Monday — concept

Multiple linear regression as Week 8's simple regression with more
than one predictor; the *"holding constant"* coefficient
interpretation; adjusted R² as a fairness tweak; common pitfalls
(multicollinearity at intro depth).

### Wednesday — application

Read a real multiple-regression output table in context: name the
response, the predictors, and what each coefficient is saying with
the other variables held constant.

### Friday — accountability

Logistic regression for a yes/no outcome: the difference between a
linear and a logistic fit, what odds and log-odds mean at intuition
depth, and how to interpret a logistic coefficient in plain English.

## Primary source basis

IMS Chapter 8 (multiple regression) and Chapter 9 (logistic
regression), extended with ISLBS Chapter 7 examples.

## Supplemental source / custom needs

Course-voice rewrites of the OpenIntro material, plus 1–2 clinical
worked examples for the logistic Friday spine.

## Learning goals

Students should be able to:

- [ ] Read a multiple-regression output table and interpret each
      coefficient *holding the other predictors constant*.
- [ ] Explain what adjusted R² adds to plain R² and when it matters.
- [ ] Recognize a multicollinearity warning at intuition depth.
- [ ] Interpret a logistic-regression coefficient as a slope on the
      log-odds scale and translate it to plain-English risk language.

## Assessment links

Links to the bank/manifests for this week will appear here once items
are built.

- Monday exit ticket: TBD
- Wednesday exit ticket: TBD
- Friday quiz: TBD
- Homework: TBD
